content:
IVX HOLDINGS — LIVE USER CAPACITY, LOAD TEST AND STRESS TEST

OBJECTIVE

Determine the real production capacity of IVX Holdings.

Do not estimate capacity from code or server size.

Run controlled tests against staging first, then production only with owner approval and safe limits.

The final report must identify:

- Maximum stable concurrent users
- Maximum requests per second
- Maximum concurrent chat users
- Maximum concurrent realtime connections
- Maximum media uploads
- Maximum AI requests
- First failure point
- Main bottleneck
- Recovery behavior
- Recommended production capacity limit

==================================================
SAFETY RULES
==================================================

1. Do not run destructive tests against production.
2. Use staging for full stress testing.
3. Use production only for limited smoke-load verification.
4. Use synthetic test accounts.
5. Do not use real customer data.
6. Do not create uncontrolled database costs.
7. Do not overload third-party services without rate-limit controls.
8. Stop the test immediately if:
   - Error rate exceeds 5%
   - Database connections exceed 85%
   - CPU exceeds 90% for 5 minutes
   - Memory exceeds 90%
   - Production health fails
   - Data integrity errors appear

==================================================
PHASE 1 — BASELINE
==================================================

Before load testing, record:

- Render instance type
- CPU
- Memory
- Database size
- Supabase connection limits
- Realtime connection limits
- API timeout
- AI provider rate limits
- Storage limits
- CDN limits
- Current average response time
- Current error rate

Return baseline evidence.

==================================================
PHASE 2 — TEST LEVELS
==================================================

Run the following concurrent-user levels:

- 10 users
- 25 users
- 50 users
- 100 users
- 250 users
- 500 users
- 1,000 users
- 2,500 users
- 5,000 users
- 10,000 users

Do not continue to the next level if the current level fails the acceptance criteria.

At every level, run for at least 10 minutes.

For the expected production level, run a 30-minute soak test.

==================================================
PHASE 3 — USER TRAFFIC MIX
==================================================

Simulate realistic activity:

- 20% login and session refresh
- 25% feed browsing
- 15% property and deal browsing
- 15% chat and realtime messages
- 10% likes, comments, follows and saves
- 5% image uploads
- 3% video uploads
- 5% search
- 2% Owner AI requests

Do not simulate only repeated /health requests.

==================================================
PHASE 4 — AUTHENTICATION LOAD
==================================================

Test:

- Concurrent registration
- Concurrent login
- Session refresh
- Password reset request
- Owner authorization
- Unauthorized route rejection
- Token expiry handling

Measure:

- Requests per second
- p50 latency
- p95 latency
- p99 latency
- Error rate
- HTTP 401/403 correctness
- HTTP 500 count

Acceptance:

- No authentication HTTP 500 errors
- p95 under 1.5 seconds
- Error rate under 1%

==================================================
PHASE 5 — FEED AND SOCIAL LOAD
==================================================

Test:

- Feed pagination
- Profile loading
- Property cards
- Reels metadata
- Likes
- Comments
- Shares
- Save post
- Follow/unfollow
- Search

Measure:

- API latency
- Database query latency
- Cache hit rate
- Slow queries
- Error rate
- Duplicate writes

Acceptance:

- p95 read latency under 1.5 seconds
- p95 write latency under 2 seconds
- No duplicate actions
- No missing records
- Error rate under 1%

==================================================
PHASE 6 — CHAT AND REALTIME LOAD
==================================================

Test separate chat levels:

- 50 concurrent connected users
- 100
- 250
- 500
- 1,000
- 2,500
- 5,000

Each connected user must:

- Join a conversation
- Send messages
- Receive messages
- Reconnect after interruption
- Update read receipt
- Send typing status

Measure:

- Concurrent WebSocket connections
- Message delivery latency
- Message loss
- Duplicate messages
- Incorrect order
- Reconnect success
- Database write rate
- Realtime disconnect rate

Acceptance:

- Message loss: 0
- Duplicate messages: 0
- p95 delivery under 2 seconds
- Reconnect success above 99%
- Error rate under 1%

==================================================
PHASE 7 — MEDIA LOAD
==================================================

Test:

- Concurrent image uploads: 10, 25, 50, 100
- Concurrent video uploads: 5, 10, 25, 50
- Thumbnail generation
- Retry after failed upload
- Video playback
- CDN delivery

Measure:

- Upload completion rate
- Upload duration
- Processing time
- Storage errors
- CDN errors
- Memory usage
- Bandwidth

Acceptance:

- No corrupt files
- Successful upload rate above 99%
- No application crash
- No database timeout

==================================================
PHASE 8 — AI LOAD
==================================================

Test controlled Owner AI requests:

- 1 concurrent request
- 5
- 10
- 25
- 50

Measure:

- Provider response time
- Gateway errors
- Rate-limit responses
- Timeout rate
- Cost per test level
- Retry behavior

Do not run unlimited AI traffic.

Acceptance:

- Correct rate-limit handling
- No secret exposure
- No cascading API failure
- Graceful response on provider limits

==================================================
PHASE 9 — SPIKE TEST
==================================================

Run a sudden traffic spike:

- 100 users to 1,000 users within 60 seconds
- Hold for 10 minutes
- Drop back to 100 users

Measure:

- Autoscaling
- CPU
- Memory
- Database connections
- Error rate
- Recovery time
- Queue buildup

==================================================
PHASE 10 — SOAK TEST
==================================================

Run the highest stable load for 4 hours.

Monitor:

- Memory growth
- Connection leaks
- Database connection leaks
- Realtime disconnects
- CPU drift
- Error growth
- Storage accumulation
- Log volume
- AI gateway stability

Acceptance:

- No memory leak
- No increasing error trend
- No connection exhaustion
- No data corruption

==================================================
PHASE 11 — FAILURE AND RECOVERY TEST
==================================================

Test recovery from:

- Render restart
- Temporary Supabase disconnect
- AI provider timeout
- Realtime reconnect
- Network interruption
- Failed upload
- Database timeout

Verify:

- Automatic recovery
- No data loss
- No duplicate messages
- No corrupted sessions
- Health endpoint returns to 200

==================================================
PHASE 12 — REQUIRED METRICS
==================================================

For every test level return:

| Concurrent Users | RPS | p50 | p95 | p99 | Errors | CPU | Memory | DB Connections | Result |

Also return:

- WebSocket connections
- Chat messages per second
- Media uploads per minute
- AI requests per minute
- Database writes per second
- Database reads per second
- Reconnect success rate

==================================================
PHASE 13 — FINAL CAPACITY REPORT
==================================================

Return:

1. Maximum stable concurrent users
2. Maximum safe production users
3. Maximum requests per second
4. Maximum realtime connections
5. Maximum messages per second
6. Maximum concurrent image uploads
7. Maximum concurrent video uploads
8. Maximum safe AI concurrency
9. First failure point
10. Primary bottleneck
11. Secondary bottleneck
12. Required infrastructure upgrade
13. Estimated capacity after upgrade
14. Recommended alert thresholds
15. Recommended autoscaling rules

Classify capacity as:

- DEVELOPMENT ONLY
- SMALL PRODUCTION
- MEDIUM PRODUCTION
- LARGE PRODUCTION
- ENTERPRISE SCALE

==================================================
FINAL RELEASE RULE
==================================================

Do not claim support for 10,000, 100,000 or 1,000,000 users without measured evidence.

Report both:

- Concurrent online users
- Total registered users

These are different measurements.

No estimate should be presented as verified capacity.
